<thought>
I am at row 02 column B facing right (`>`). The cell immediately in front of me (row 02 column C) is lava (`L`), which is deadly, so I cannot move forward. To find a safe path, I need to turn and find a direction that avoids lava. Turning down (facing down `v`) looks promising, as row 03 column B is safe (`.`). To turn from right (`>`) to down (`v`), I will turn right.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am currently at row 02, column B facing down (`v`). The goal is at row 08, column H. Moving forward down into row 03 column B would be safe since it is `.`. Heading straight down first is a good approach since it avoids lava cells on immediate sides. I will move forward.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am currently at row 03, column B, facing down (`v`). The next cell in front (row 04, column B) is a floor cell (.), which is safe to move into. I want to move downwards first since the goal is located on row 08, column H, so moving forward is the right choice for now.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am currently at row 04, column B, facing down (`v`). The goal is at row 08, column H. Moving forward (down) will lead me into a lava cell (row 05, column B is `.` but row 07 has lava blocking below, so direct forward movement is risky). To navigate safely, I should turn right to face left (`<`) or left to face right (`>`). Turning left to face right will allow me to move across column C avoiding lava and get closer to the goal on the right side of the map.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I am at row 04, column B, facing right (`>`). The cell directly ahead at row 04, column C contains lava (`L`), which is deadly and must be avoided. Therefore, moving forward is not safe. I should change my direction to find a safe path. I can turn left to face up and check if that way is safe or better to explore to reach the goal at row 08, column H.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I am currently at row 04, column B facing up (`^`). The goal is at row 08, column H. Moving forward from my current position will place me into lava (`L`) at row 03, column B, which is deadly. Therefore, I need to change my orientation to avoid lava. Turning right will make me face right (`>`), which leads to safe cells at row 04, columns C and beyond. So I will TURN_RIGHT.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am currently at row 04, column B, facing right (`>`). The goal is at row 08, column H. The direct path forward is blocked by lava at C4. I need to avoid lava and find a safe way downwards. The cells ahead (C4) is lava, so I should turn downwards by turning right twice to face down (`v`) and then move forward to navigate down safely.
</thought>
<action>

From facing right ('>'), turning right will make me face down ('v').

<thought>
I am currently at row 04 column B facing down (`v`). The goal is at row 08 column H. Moving forward will take me into lava at row 05 column B, which is unsafe. I need to turn to avoid lava and find a safe path. Turning right will orient me to face left (`<`), which leads to a wall, so not an option. Turning left will face me right (`>`), which is towards column C, but that cell has lava. Instead, I will turn left twice or turn right once and then move to explore a safe path. The best immediate safe move is to turn left to face right (`>`).
</thought>
<action>

</action>

<thought>

Turning up (`^`) would direct me into lava as well. Turning right to face down (`v`) would keep me safe and allow me to move forward into an empty cell at (05, B). So I will turn right to face down.
</thought>

<thought>
